violations.description: Food supplies. — --Unknown source for vacuum sealed organ meats in freezer.

Preventing contamination by employees. — --Employee observed handling ready-to-eat food with bare hands. -Upon arrival, observed staff handling ready to eat romaine lettuce,placing into bowl without gloves for customer salad.
Variance requirement for specialized processing methods. — ---Variety of jars of jelly throughout kitchen refrigeration. -Bottled (in-house?) grape juice with date of October 2025.

General equipment and utensils - Materials and design. — --Material of food contact surface was not constructed or designed to be smooth, nonabsorbent and cleanable. -Small wood cutting board is cracked in 2-3 places.

General equipment and utensils - Maintenance and operation. — --Food contact equipment/utensil was not maintained in good repair or sound condition. -Ice machine interior panel held up with plastic bottle used as brace.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Food contact surface was not cleaned adequately. -Residue/build up at food surfaces of flat sandwich press, pannini press and food rack in oven.--Food contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. -Frost build up at interior surfaces of chest freezer used for cooling foods.

Employee dressing areas and lockers. — --employees' clothing/possessions were in an area where contamination of food, equipment, utensils, linens or single-service items may occur. -Personal food items (open kombucha bottles) store commingling with food service items in two refrigerators.--In-use apron stored on food service rack at entry to kitchen.
General premises. — --Recyclables are over-flowing bin in dry storage area.

Handling and storage of poisonous and toxic materials. — --Secondary container of poisonous or toxic material was not clearly identified to indicated contents. unlabeled spray bottles. -Unknown product in spray bottles with clear liquid and blue liquid at dirty side of dish pit.

Food display and service. — --Potentially hazardous food was not kept at an internal temperature of 41F or below during display or service. -Cream cheese frosting found at 76F storged above refrigerator in kitchen area. -Ham at top of containers in table top cold hold unit found at 56F (38F at bottom). (Recommend smaller portion in unit and to defrost bottom interior so containers stay below cold lin.) -Back room left refrigerator found with thermometer showing 52F, Quinoa salad at 46f. (Unit had been opened frequently for stocking . Directed staff to move product to different unit or to add ice if refrigerator does not recover to 41F or less within half hour.)
General employee practices. — --Food employee observed without proper hair restraint. -Staff doing kitchen prep has long beard and was not wearing a beard net.

Preventing contamination from equipment, utensils, and wiping cloths. — --Cloth for wiping food spills was not stored in sanitizing solution (at proper concentration) between uses. -Cloth used at espresso machine is not kept in sani bucket between uses and is exchanged approximately every four hours.

Date marking and disposition of ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food. — --Refrigerated, ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food (prepared more than 24 hours ago) was not properly date marked. -Burritos in glass door refrigerator were not dated with thaw date. -Shredded pork in back refrigerator was not dated with thaw date.

Plumbing system. — --Plumbing system was not designed, constructed, operated and/or maintained according to regulation. -Dishwasher has leak. *Facility is working with service company for 3rd time to fix issue.

Cleaning and maintenance of floors, walls and ceilings. — --Physical facilities (e.g. floors, walls, ceiling etc.…) were not clean or not cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. -Debris/residue at floor edges around equipment and below dish pit. -Water below floor tiles at entrance to kitchen area (due to dishwasher leak).
Clean, employee dressing area, cleaning equipment storage, living/sleeping quarters separate (1 pt deducted)
Clean, proper use, storage, sanitizing solution (2 pts deducted)
Hot holding and cold holding temperatures maintained, unwrapped food not reserved (5 pts deducted)
Clean, proper use, storage, sanitizing solution (2 pts deducted)
Hot holding and cold holding temperatures maintained, unwrapped food not reserved (5 pts deducted)
Method, temperature and time (5 pts deducted)
Date marking accomplished, discarded (2 pts deducted)
Designed, constructed, installed, and maintained (1 pt deducted)
Cleaning frequency, maintained clean (2 pts deducted)
Thermometers provided, used, clean, and accurate (1 pt deducted)
